message:
fix: Handle large HTTP request bodies for Claude Code

Claude Code sends large requests (50KB+ with system prompts). The
previous implementation only read 8KB which truncated requests.

Changes:
- Read HTTP headers first (up to 16KB)
- Parse Content-Length header properly
- Dynamically allocate and read full body based on Content-Length
- Handle partial reads until complete body is received

This fixes the "400 terminated" error when using Claude Code.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1 @@
+95291
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+{
+  "ABIRoot": {
+    "kind": "Root",
+    "name": "NO_MODULE",
+    "printedName": "NO_MODULE",
+    "json_format_version": 8
+  },
+  "ConstValues": []
+}
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
+<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
+<plist version="1.0">
+<dict>
+	<key>com.apple.security.get-task-allow</key>
+	<true/>
+</dict>
+</plist>

@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+import Foundation
+
+extension Foundation.Bundle {
+    static let module: Bundle = {
+        let mainPath = Bundle.main.bundleURL.appendingPathComponent("ZellApp_ZellApp.bundle").path
+        let buildPath = "/Users/steven_chong/Downloads/repos/zell/app/.build/arm64-apple-macosx/debug/ZellApp_ZellApp.bundle"
+
+        let preferredBundle = Bundle(path: mainPath)
+
+        guard let bundle = preferredBundle ?? Bundle(path: buildPath) else {
+            // Users can write a function called fatalError themselves, we should be resilient against that.
+            Swift.fatalError("could not load resource bundle: from \(mainPath) or \(buildPath)")
+        }
+
+        return bundle
+    }()
+}
\ No newline at end of file
